name: Key Lime Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: Unsalted butter, melted and cooled (1/4 cup)
Cream cheese, softened (2 1/2 packages, 8 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the crust: In a bowl, stir together the graham cracker crumbs and sugar. Add the melted and cooled butter, stirring well to combine.
2. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom and 1/2 inch up the sides of a buttered 10-inch springform pan. Bake in the center of a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 8 minutes. Transfer the pan to a rack and set aside to cool.
3. For the cake: In a bowl with an electric mixer, beat together the cream cheese and sugar until smooth. Beat in the sour cream and flour, then add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Add the lime juice, vanilla, and food coloring, and beat until the mixture is smooth. Pour the filling over the crust.
5. Bake the cheesecake at 375°F (190°C) for 15 minutes. Reduce the temperature to 250°F (120°C) and bake for an additional 50 to 55 minutes, or until the center is barely set. It will continue to set as it cools.
6. Let the cake cool on a rack, then chill it, covered, overnight.
7. Remove the cheesecake from the pan and transfer it to a cake stand. With a pastry bag fitted with a decorative tip, pipe whipped cream into rosettes on the cake. Garnish with mint sprigs and lime slices.
